preview_text:
The model favors Ecuador to win at 55%, substantially ahead of Ivory Coast's 17% win probability. This represents a clear underdog positioning for Ivory Coast, though the model does assign a meaningful 28% chance to a draw outcome, suggesting competitive balance in avoiding a decisive result.

The 55–17 split indicates the model expects Ecuador to control much of the match likelihood, yet the elevated draw probability—nearly one-third of outcomes—points to a competitive fixture where neither side is forecast to run away with it. A draw remains plausible; Ecuador's advantage is real but not overwhelming in a single-match contest.

The market consensus diverges noticeably from the model. Sportsbooks and Polymarket both price Ecuador's win probability around 39%, a gap of roughly 16 percentage points below the model's 55%. Conversely, both assign Ivory Coast a 27% win chance versus the model's 17%, and place draws near 34% against the model's 28%. The dashboard does not adjudicate which source is more reliable; the divergence reflects genuine disagreement on Ecuador's true edge in this fixture.
